creator: ambrisko@whistle.com
text:
Can't netboot elf kernels this let's it happen

Fix: ambrisko writes:
| Robert Nordier writes:
| | When an ELF kernel becomes standard, I'd be strongly in favor of
| | moving even existing code like dosboot into ports.  There is
| | precedence for this: mkisofs, for instance.
| 
| That sounds reasonable, I guess I could make the etherboot stuff into
| a port and put the patches in there for loading FreeBSD aout/elf kernels.

Attached is a uuencoding tarball of the etherboot stuff.  I created the 
tarball by taring from "/usr/ports" since it includes a change to 
linux_devel port.  This change replaced /compat/linux/usr/bin/ld with
a perl script that run /compat/linux/usr/bin/ld.linux and then runs
brandelf after the link edit so that static bins that the etherboot
package makes can immediately run.

The etherboot package builds under the linux emulation & linux devel
package.  Some of the patches I'm going to send back to the author since
they are bug fixes (the stack and data areas can collide).

Also note that it tftp's the kernel.

Doug A.
